blockers: No blocking audit blocker. Task tool runs were synchronous in this environment, so background agent ids were not exposed for launch_started metadata.
consensus_notes: Bounded-spawning targets and max-depth=3 are clearly documented in commander/capsule material.
Leaf-only worker behavior is present, but enforcement is primarily prompt/tool-manifest based rather than hard sandboxing.
Runtime and lint coverage lag the documented policy in a few failure-path areas.
conflict_notes: Commander instructions present strong Depth Guard rules, while SKILL.md itself lacks a direct depth counter or explicit recursion guard implementation.
Lint coverage validates positive markers but does not fully assert banned-model rejection or commander-recursion negatives.
main_findings: Worker leaf lock exists but is not fully tool-enforced because bash remains available.
Commander file documents bounded spawning, depth cap, and partial handling clearly.
SKILL.md relies on commander enforcement instead of implementing its own explicit depth guard.
depth-guard lint coverage misses some negative assertions for banned models/recursion.
Launcher failure handling/teardown shows possible gaps for mid-run spawn failure escalation and nested cleanup.

findings: Leaf-lock is explicit via 'DO NOT attempt to spawn sub-agents... any other agents' and 'You are a LEAF NODE'.
Worker frontmatter omits task/ask_user tools, but bash remains available so anti-spawn protection is instruction-level rather than sandbox-enforced.
Task loop is single-agent and contains no delegation workflow.
Commander file explicitly bounds SS-250 rollout to 50 squad leads x 5 workers and caps max depth at 3.
Commander-only spawning boundary and required leaf-node block for workers are documented.
Partial/failure reporting for launch shortfall is explicitly required.
SKILL.md applies bounded spawning caps but does not implement an explicit depth counter or Depth Guard algorithm.
The skill includes the task tool and relies on stampede-commander for downstream depth enforcement.
Anti-runaway landmines cover requeue/autopilot liveness more than recursion depth.
Lint script checks worker leaf-only markers, can_launch=false variants, and several commander bounded-spawning markers.
Banned-model coverage is indirect/positive-only and lacks explicit rejection assertions for banned model names.
Commander recursion prevention is mostly phrase-based rather than negative lint coverage.
Launcher banned-model validation appears startup-only; worker/child launch failures do not clearly trigger fleet-wide halt.
Teardown kills tracked PIDs but may not recursively clean nested child agents, leaving orphan risk.
Preflight validates access/readiness but does not fully pre-validate later child-model launch authorization; partial status is recorded more than actively enforced.
